---
title: "Global Health Spending and Maternal Mortality Dashboard" 
author: "VIOLET ESSIEN"
format: dashboard
---
```{python}
#| include: false
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from itables import show
import country_converter as coco
import plotly.graph_objects as go
```
```{python}
#| include: false
health_pct = pd.read_csv("government_health_spending_of_total_gov_spending_percent.csv")
```
```{python}
#| include: false
mmr = pd.read_csv("mmr_ihme.csv")
```
```{python}
#| include: false
health_pct_clean = health_pct.rename(columns={'name': 'country'})
```
```{python}
#| include: false
health_pct_long = health_pct_clean.melt(
    id_vars=['country', 'geo'],   
    var_name='year',        
    value_name='spending' 
)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
health_pct_long['year'] = health_pct_long['year'].astype(int)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
health_pct_long["country"] = health_pct_long["country"].replace(
    {"UAE": "United Arab Emirates"}
)
health_pct_long["country_code"] = coco.convert(
    health_pct_long["country"], to="ISO3")
```
```{python}
#| include: false
health_pct_long_2000_2020 = health_pct_long[
    (health_pct_long['year'] >= 2000) & (health_pct_long['year'] <= 2020)
]
```
```{python}
#| include: false
health_pct_filtered = health_pct_long_2000_2020[
    health_pct_long_2000_2020['country'].isin(
        ['Germany', 'Nigeria', 'Switzerland', 'Kenya', 'India', 'United States of America', 'Italy']
    )
]
```
```{python}
#| include: false
health_pct_long_2018 = health_pct_long.query("year == 2018")

highest_row = health_pct_long_2018.sort_values("spending", ascending=False).head(1).squeeze()
highest_spending_country = highest_row["country"]
highest_spending_value = round(highest_row["spending"], 1)

lowest_row = health_pct_long_2018.sort_values("spending", ascending=True).head(1).squeeze()
lowest_spending_country = lowest_row["country"]
lowest_spending_value = round(lowest_row["spending"], 1)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
health_pct_long['continent'] = coco.convert(
    names=health_pct_long['country'],
    to='continent')
```
```{python}
#| include: false
avg_hlthpct = health_pct_long.groupby('continent')['spending'].mean().reset_index()
avg_hlthpct.columns = ['Continent', 'Avg Spending (%)']
avg_hlthpct['Avg Spending (%)'] = avg_hlthpct['Avg Spending (%)'].round(2)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
global_avg_spending = round(health_pct_long['spending'].mean(), 1)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
top_10_df = health_pct_long.query("year == 2019").nlargest(10, 'spending')

fig_table = go.Figure(data=[go.Table(
    header=dict(
        values=['<b>Country</b>', '<b>Health Expenditure (%)</b>'],
        fill_color='indigo',
        font=dict(color='white', size=12),
        align='left'
    ),
    cells=dict(
        values=[top_10_df.country, top_10_df.spending.round(2)],
        fill_color='white',
        align='left'
    )
)])

fig_table.update_layout(
    margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
    height=300
)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
fig_chart = px.line(
    health_pct_filtered,
    x='year',
    y='spending',
    color='country',
    title='Health Spending by Country Trend (2000-2020)',
    labels={
        'country': 'Country',
        'spending': 'Health Spending (%)',
        'year': 'Year'
    }
)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
fig_map = px.choropleth(
    health_pct_long_2000_2020,
    locations="country_code",
    color="spending",
    hover_name="country",
    animation_frame="year",
    title="Government Health Spending of Total Government Spending (2000-2020)",
)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
mmr_raw = pd.read_csv("mmr_ihme.csv")
mmr_data = mmr_raw.rename(columns={'name': 'country'})

mmr_long = mmr_data.melt(
    id_vars=['country', 'geo'],   
    var_name='year',        
    value_name='mortality_rate' 
)

mmr_long['continent'] = coco.convert(names=mmr_long['country'], to='continent')

mmr_long["country"] = mmr_long["country"].replace({"UAE": "United Arab Emirates"})
mmr_long["country_code"] = coco.convert(mmr_long["country"], to="ISO3")
mmr_long['year'] = mmr_long['year'].astype(int)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
mmr_long_country = mmr_long[
    mmr_long['country'].isin(
        ['China', 'Nigeria', 'Japan', 'Ghana', 'United Kingdom', 'Yemen', 'New Zealand']
    )
]

fig_chart_mmr = px.line(
    mmr_long_country,
    x='year',
    y='mortality_rate',
    color='country',
    title='Maternal Mortality Rate by Country Trend',
    labels={
        'country': 'Country',
        'mortality_rate': 'Maternal Mortality Rate',
        'year': 'Year'
    }
)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
fig_bubble_map = px.scatter_geo(
    mmr_long,
    locations="country_code",
    color="mortality_rate", 
    hover_name="country", 
    size="mortality_rate", 
    animation_frame="year",
    projection="natural earth",
    title="Global Maternal Mortality Crisis Trend (Bubble View)",
    color_continuous_scale=px.colors.sequential.OrRd,
    size_max=30
)
fig_bubble_map.update_geos(showcountries=True, countrycolor="LightGrey")
```
```{python}
#| include: false
top_10_mmr = mmr_long.query("year == 2019").nlargest(10, 'mortality_rate')

fig_table_mmr = go.Figure(data=[go.Table(
    header=dict(
        values=['<b>Country</b>', '<b>Mortality Rate</b>'],
        fill_color='indigo',
        font=dict(color='white', size=12),
        align='left'
    ),
    cells=dict(
        values=[top_10_mmr.country, top_10_mmr.mortality_rate.round(1)],
        fill_color='pink',
        align='left'
    )
)])

# ✅ Fixed: was incorrectly updating fig_table before
fig_table_mmr.update_layout(
    margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10),
    height=300
)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
hlthpct_mmr = pd.merge(
    health_pct_long,
    mmr_long,
    on=['continent', 'country', 'country_code', 'year'],
    how='inner' 
)

hlthpct_mmr['efficiency'] = hlthpct_mmr['spending'] / hlthpct_mmr['mortality_rate']
```
```{python}
#| include: false
fig_bubble = px.scatter(
    hlthpct_mmr.query("year == 2018"),
    x="spending",
    y="mortality_rate",
    size="mortality_rate",
    color="continent",
    hover_name="country_code",
    title="Health Spending vs Maternal Mortality (2018)",
    labels={
        "spending": "Health Spending (%)",
        "mortality_rate": "Maternal Mortality Ratio"
    }
)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
# ✅ Renamed to fig_box_mmr to avoid conflict with fig_box in the relationship tab
fig_box_mmr = px.box(
    hlthpct_mmr.query("year == 2018"),
    x="continent",
    y="mortality_rate",
    color="continent",
    title="Maternal Mortality Distribution by Continent (2018)",
    labels={
        "mortality_rate": "Maternal Mortality Rate (MMR)",
        "continent": "Continent"
    }
)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
region_summary = hlthpct_mmr.groupby(['continent', 'year']).agg({
    'spending': 'mean',
    'mortality_rate': 'mean'
}).reset_index()

fig_trend = px.line(
    region_summary,
    x="year",
    y="mortality_rate",
    color="continent",
    title="MMR Trends by Continent Over Time",
    labels={
        'continent': 'Continent',
        'mortality_rate': 'Mortality Rate'
    }
)

fig_compare = px.bar(
    region_summary.query("year == 2018"),
    x="continent",
    y="spending",
    color="continent",
    title="Average Health Spending by Continent (2018)",
    labels={
        'continent': 'Continent',
        'spending': 'Avg Spending (%)'
    }
)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
top_data = hlthpct_mmr.query("year == 2018").nlargest(10, "spending")

top_hlthmmr = px.bar(
    top_data,
    x="spending",
    y="country",
    color="mortality_rate",
    orientation="h",
    title="Top Spending Countries with MMR Overlay (2018)",
    labels={
        'spending': 'Health Spending (%)',
        'country': 'Country',
        'mortality_rate': 'Maternal Mortality Ratio'
    }
)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
fig_box = px.box(
    hlthpct_mmr.query("year == 2018"),
    x="continent",
    y="spending",
    color="continent",
    title="Health Spending Distribution by Continent (2018)",
    labels={
        "spending": "Health Spending (%)",
        "continent": "Continent"
    }
)

fig_cont = px.scatter(
    hlthpct_mmr,
    x="spending",
    y="mortality_rate",
    color="continent",
    title="Spending vs Mortality by Continent (All Years)",
    labels={
        "spending": "Health Spending (%)",
        "mortality_rate": "Maternal Mortality Ratio"
    }
)
```

# Government Health Spending Percent of Overall Expenditure

## Row {height=20%}

::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.valuebox icon="cash-icon" color="#1E90FF" title="Highest Health Spending 2018"}
`{python} str(highest_spending_value)`
`{python} highest_spending_country`
:::
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.valuebox icon="thermometer" color="#87CEFA" title="Lowest Health Spending 2018"}
`{python} str(lowest_spending_value)`
`{python} lowest_spending_country`
:::
:::

::: {.column width="34%"}
::: {.valuebox icon="bar-chart" color="#104160" title="Global Average Health Expenditure"}
`{python} str(global_avg_spending)`
:::
:::

## Row {height=45%}

::: {.column width="100%"}
```{python}
fig_map.show()
```
:::

## Row {height=35%}

::: {.column width="65%"}
```{python}
fig_chart.show()
```
:::

::: {.column width="35%"}
```{python}
fig_table.show()
```
```{python}
show(avg_hlthpct)
```
:::

# Maternal Mortality Rate

## Row {height=50%}

::: {.column width="60%"}
```{python}
fig_bubble_map.show()
```
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
```{python}
fig_chart_mmr.show()
```
:::

## Row {height=50%}

::: {.column width="60%"}
```{python}
fig_box_mmr.show()
```
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
```{python}
fig_table_mmr.show()
```
:::

# Relationship: Health Spending vs Maternal Mortality

## Row {height=50%}

::: {.column width="60%"}
```{python}
fig_bubble.show()
```
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
```{python}
fig_trend.show()
```
:::

## Row {height=25%}

::: {.column width="60%"}
```{python}
fig_box.show()
```
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
```{python}
fig_compare.show()
```
:::

## Row {height=25%}

::: {.column width="60%"}
```{python}
top_hlthmmr.show()
```
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
```{python}
fig_cont.show()
```
:::

# About Data

The aim of choosing these indicators is evaluating the Impact of Government Health Expenditure on Global Maternal Mortality. This project serves as the capstone for the GRAPH Course, demonstrating a comprehensive end-to-end data science workflow.

**SDG Target 3.1** aims to reduce the global maternal mortality ratio to less than 70 per 100,000 live births by 2030.

This dashboard explores: *How efficiently do different nations convert public health spending into the preservation of maternal lives?*
